chapter_title.en: The Thirty-Eighth Assembly, the Assembly of Tuesday, the Fourth of Safar, 368 AH.

translations.bn: Ahmad b. Ziyad b. Ja`far al-Hamadani (rh) narrated to us. He said: `Ali b. Ibrahim b. Hashim narrated to us. He said: Ja`far b. Salama al-Azhari narrated to us from Ibrahim b. Muhammad

translations.bn: al-Thaqafi. He said: al-`Abbas b. Bakkar narrated to us. He said: `Abd al-Wahid b. Abi `Amr narrated to us from al-Kalbi from Abi Salih from Abi Hurayra from the Messenger of Allah (s). He said: On the Throne, it is written: “I am Allah. There is no god but Me. I am Alone without any partners. Muhammad is My Servant and My Messenger, and I have aided him with `Ali.” So, Allah revealed, “It is He who supported you with His help and with the believers.” [8:62] The “help” is `Ali (as), who [also] entered among the believers; so, he (as) is on both sides together.
translations.de: al-Thaqafi. He said: al-`Abbas b. Bakkar narrated to us. He said: `Abd al-Wahid b. Abi `Amr narrated to us from al-Kalbi from Abi Salih from Abi Hurayra from Messenger of Allah (s). He said: On Throne, it is written: “I am Allah. There is no god but Me. I am Alone without any partners. Muhammad is My Servant and My Messenger, and I have aided him with `Ali.” So, Allah revealed, “It is He who supported you with His help and with believers.” [8:62] “help” is `Ali (as), who [also] entered among believers; so, he (as) is on both sides together. [Übersetzung]

narrators.known_identity: Ahmad ibn Ziyad ibn Ja'far al-Hamadani
Abu Hurayra, companion of the Prophet

key_phrases.phrase_en: Muhammad is My servant and My messenger

seo_questions.bn: What does this hadith from al-Amali al-Saduq teach? [bn]

summaries.en: Abu Hurayra narrates that the Prophet (SAWW) said that inscribed on the Divine Throne is: 'I am Allah, there is no god but Me, I am Alone without partners, Muhammad is My servant and My messenger, and I have aided him with Ali.' Allah then revealed Quran 8:62, identifying 'the help' as Ali (AS), who also enters among 'the believers', thus fulfilling both roles simultaneously. This narration connects the pre-eternal divine decree with the Quranic revelation about Ali's role.
summaries.ur: ابو ہریرہ رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ وسلم سے روایت کرتے ہیں کہ عرش پر لکھا ہے: میں اللہ ہوں، محمد میرے بندے ہیں، میں نے انہیں علی سے مدد دی۔ پھر آیت ۸:۶۲ نازل ہوئی جس میں 'نصر' سے مراد علی علیہ السلام ہیں۔

parts.text: حدثنا أحمد بن زياد بن جعفر الهمداني (رحمه الله)، قال

text: : حدثنا علي بن إبراهيم بن هاشم، قال: حدثنا جعفر بن سلمة الاهوازي، عن إبراهيم بن محمد الثقفي، قال: حدثنا العباس بن بكار، قال: حدثنا عبد الواحد بن أبي عمرو، عن الكلبي، عن أبي صالح، عن أبي هريرة، عن رسول الله (صلى الله عليه وآله)، قال: مكتوب على العرش: أنا الله لا إله إلا أنا، وحدي لا شريك لي، ومحمد عبدي ورسولي، أيدته بعلي. فأنزل الله عز وجل (هو الذي أيدك بنصره وبالمؤمنين) (2) فكان النصر عليا (عليه السلام)، ودخل مع المؤمنين، فدخل في الوجهين جميعا (صلى الله عليه) (3).

en.bilal-muhammad: Ahmad b. Ziyad b. Ja`far al-Hamadani (rh) narrated to us. He said: `Ali b. Ibrahim b. Hashim narrated to us. He said: Ja`far b. Salama al-Azhari narrated to us from Ibrahim b. Muhammad al-Thaqafi. He said: al-`Abbas b. Bakkar narrated to us. He said: `Abd al-Wahid b. Abi `Amr narrated to us from al-Kalbi from Abi Salih from Abi Hurayra from the Messenger of Allah (s). He said: On the Throne, it is written: “I am Allah. There is no god but Me. I am Alone without any partners. Muhammad is My Servant and My Messenger, and I have aided him with `Ali.” So, Allah revealed, “It is He who supported you with His help and with the believers.” [8:62] The “help” is `Ali (as), who [also] entered among the believers; so, he (as) is on both sides together.
